scores.one_line_justification: Correct on every rule interaction (quiet-hours flush deferral, throttle hold, R2 order, R3/R7 precedence) with exact-id tests and a clean dict-queue design, but docs/SPEC.md was never extended to document the new digest requirement (CR and docstrings carry it instead).

Full spec fidelity including a faithful new R11 recording the silent quiet-hours hold; flat-list store mirrors the existing deferred pattern; tests are solid but use a weaker sorted-order assertion and never pin what happens to a pending batch after switching back to immediate.

Strongest interaction handling (audits quiet-hours flush suppression within R9's vocabulary, re-routes deferrals into the digest after an opt-in, defensively sorts for R2) with tests pinning each, but docs/SPEC.md gained no R11 and the mode vocabulary is duplicated across routing.py and cli.py.

Clean minimal change with a comprehensive R11 and honest docstrings; all interactions correct at flush time, but the test set is the thinnest of the five and the documented switching-back-keeps-pending-digest decision has no test.

Behaviour matches its amended spec including quiet-hours and throttle holds, with strong exact-id and switch-back tests, but it invents a queued_digest audit reason the ticket never asked for (recorded, yet it expands R9's closed vocabulary), leaves the Delivery docstring stale, and hardcodes mode choices in the CLI.
